title: Schradera angustifolia
taxonomy: Schradera angustifolia is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family comprises approximately 13,000 to 15,000 species of flowering plants, commonly known as the madder family. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families. The classification of Schradera angustifolia also places it within the tribe Rubieae and the subfamily Rubioideae. Further classification details are necessary for a more comprehensive understanding of this species.

intro: Schradera angustifolia, also known as the "bitter Schradera", is a type of plant in the Rubiaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Schradera angustifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
